reasoning.grounding: Claims are tied accurately to the denial text and the patient's case facts; no external citations or corpus available, which caps this dimension at 3. No fabricated grounding.
reasoning.appeal_vector_capture: Squarely identifies and attacks both decisive flaws: the denial's silence on appeal rights/deadlines/process, and the fact that the denial is administrative while the clinical picture warrants a medical-necessity review now.
reasoning.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Uses this patient's specific documented facts (knee replacement, post-op infection, delayed rehab, ongoing mobility deficits) directly against the plan's medical-necessity-review pathway rather than generic assertions.
reasoning.evidence_completeness: Marshals the right available evidence and supplies an accurate, targeted missing-evidence checklist covering plan language, operative/infection records, PT progress notes, a medical-necessity letter, visit records, the original authorization scope, and the appeal-process details.
reasoning.persuasive_coherence: Tight, calm, well-structured: states the ground, concedes the procedural gap honestly while reframing it as administrative, anchors the medical-necessity request, and asserts review rights. No padding.

improvement_notes.grounding: Where a supporting corpus or plan-document citations are available, anchor each key assertion to a specific source reference so claims are independently verifiable rather than resting on narrative alone.
improvement_notes.appeal_vector_capture: Continue isolating the single most decisive basis the denial rests on and attacking it head-on; pairing a procedural-fairness gap with the substantive merits is an effective two-track structure to retain.
improvement_notes.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Keep mapping each cited patient fact to the exact criterion the denial invokes; consider adding measurable functional metrics (range of motion, gait, pain scores) when available to make necessity even more concrete.
improvement_notes.evidence_completeness: Strong checklist; to strengthen further, briefly note which listed items the member can obtain immediately versus which must come from the provider, and tie each item to the specific point it would prove.
improvement_notes.persuasive_coherence: Maintain the concede-and-reframe pattern and the clear request at the close; keep paragraphs single-purpose and avoid restating the same point across sections.
